replies.title: Re: Spengler on Time, Destiny, Space, and Motion and Divination Offer
replies.content:
Okay, your Kittenness, here is what the Runes told me. If you have follow up questions, I've updated the settings on my journal and have had some anonymous folks successfully post today.

I used a "Wyrd, Werthende, Sculd" spread, which is the Anglo-Saxon version of "Urdh, Verdhandi, Skuld", who are the three Norns, usually seen as being equivalent to the fates and thus, roughly "Past, Present, and Future." There's a bit of nuance, but the most important thing is that Sculd/Skuld is etymologically related to "should" - so rather than "this will happen", the future indicated is what "should" happen given how things stand now.

I shook the runes, cast them into a dice tray, and then selected one for each with my eyes closed. Here's what I cast/drew:

Draw
Wyrd (Past): Laguz
Werthende (Present): Eihwaz
Sculd (Future): Fehu (Murky, which means face down)

Interpretation
This reading is a bit rough. Laguz, which means "Lake" or "Sea" and shares much of the symbolism common to large bodies of water indicates you've been undergoing some hard challenges or tests, maybe transformative ones. From Eihwaz, which is the Yew and the World Tree, it seems that from those tests, you have found discipline, most likely of a mental or spiritual kind, and that is the key note for what will happen/what you will do this week. If you are on any kind of initiatory path, it may indicate a step onward or upward there, and in any case, it points to a link between "above" and "below", and potentially a transition between them. Looking forward, though, we have Fehu, which means "Fee" and is linked with money, but also with any kind of "power" that gets its strength through exchange or interaction - so, money, favors, social relationships and the like. Murky can mean one of two things - either "overdoing" what Fehu represents or else a blockage of it. Given the tenor of the rest of the reading, I'm inclined to think this doesn't straightforwardly mean money troubles - rather, I think it might be that whatever strength or creativity has come to you through the trials and discipline mentioned above has a risk of burning you out. So consider finding ways to "keep some gas in the tank" as you go forward past this week.

I hope this helps, and as I said, please let me know any follow up questions you have!

comments.title: Spagyrics
comments.content:
What are your recommended books/resources for learning more about spagyrics? I've successfully made one spagyric tincture (lemon balm) and am excited to do more!

And is there any reason why astragalus root would be unsuitable to use for a spagyric tincture?

replies.title: Re: Spagyrics
replies.content: The best resources I know of are the book Spagyrics by Manfred Junius, on the one hand, and the correspondence courses written by Jean Dubuis, which were made freely downloadable in his will and can be found here. As for astragalus, as far as I know, it should be fine; any nontoxic herb or tree can be used as the basis for a spagyric preparation.

I keep seeing triple digits when I look at the time - e.g., 4:44 or 2:22 etc. This has been happening for weeks. It's like there's something compelling me to look at my watch at that particular time.

Is there any significance to triple digits like this?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Whatever significance they have will be personal to you. The best way to figure out what they're trying to say -- or what your subconscious mind is trying to say by directing your attention to them -- is to do some journaling. Write down the number, and then have a written conversation with it; ask it what it's trying to communicate to you, and then write down whatever comes to mind, no matter how odd -- don't edit or censor anything. Continue the conversation as long as seems useful. Repeat with other numbers.

comments.content:
I've had a repeated, terrible experience where my spouse will become angry with me and give me a frightening look like a demon glare. For a day or two afterward I will hear her in my head screaming at me. It gets so bad that when I am on the road I will find myself shouting back and swerving erratically into traffic.

Last time it happened it felt like there was a demon tearing at me, and I was overcome with the desire to crash my car. As the day went on I became extremely ill. When I got home she was upset that I was so sick because, she said, this was the first good day she had had in months, and any time she feels good I always try to drag her down. As far as I know, I do nothing of the kind and wish she would be in a better mood more often.

Do these things have an occult or magical cause, in your view? Or am I simply going crazy? If an occult cause, what can be done? -- I've tried the LBRP and it seems totally ineffective in this case.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: It's probably not occult in nature, but that doesn't mean you're going crazy -- just that you have a lot of unresolved psychological issues around anger. That's quite common these days. I'd encourage you to consider the Order of Spiritual Alchemy exercises, which you can find here; they've helped a lot of people, including me, to deal with unresolved issues and traumas from the past, so they don't mess up the present and the future.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
I’m sorry to hear about your situation. That is not an easy situation to deal with. From the little you described it sounds to me like your spouse might be emotionally abusive. I was in such a relationship once, with my ex-wife.

You might try reading Psychic Self-Defense by Dion Fortune, which might help you from a magical perspective. Or, from a modern psychologist, Emotional Blackmail by Susan Forward. If your situation is anything like mine was, you might not want to let your spouse see that you’re reading them.

I wish you the best of luck in finding out the cause of and a solution to this problem.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Oof, that sounds horrible to have to deal with! If all of that feels like a sudden change, as in "this is not the person I married," you may have already hit the nail directly on the head. Over the past few years, I've noticed an unusual number of people, previously demon-free, finally breaking down and inviting a demon to take up residence inside them. It's really distressing to witness 40, 50, and 60-year-olds succumbing, after a lifetime of successfully resisting demonic enticements. Of course, there's not much anyone can do to help them stay strong and defended once they've allowed a demon to ensconce within them — the damage has already been done at that point.

In some cases exorcism can rescue them, but, if the victim continues to feel as helplessly overwhelmed as they did before, they will usually just end up inviting another false-promising demon inside themselves. The type of demons that can infest humans will always try to target the people they perceive as being most at risk. I'm not sure what those demons are picking up on in the unseen, but we must glow like beacons to them when we're particularly vulnerable. Any homeless demons will always promise their struggling victims potency, partnership, and dream fulfillment, while they always end up delivering impotency, isolation, and living nightmares.

Tulpas are probably the most well-known of the human-infesting demons, and certainly the most warm-fuzzy friendly in their sales pitch. The bizarre Furry movement is utterly riddled with alter-ego, sex-kitten tulpas, promising whatever dream fantasy sexlife their victims believed they themselves couldn't achieve. That's what happens when desperate, dissatisfied, and cluelessly untrained novices begin dabbling in dangerous magical practices, without ever realizing that's what they're doing. Certain magical rituals are actually difficult to distinguish from fetish cosplay, so the mistake is understandable, though still tragic.

Those sexy/cuddly tulpas do initially apply their power to delivering on some version of their promises right up until their victim becomes thoroughly hooked and trapped. That's when all their promised dreams turn into hellish nightmares. Alas, very few people are strong enough to escape from their demon's clutches that late in the game. In the scariest cases, the pattern that was the victim’s persona gets permanently locked away, as the demon completely takes over. Sex tulpas are very good at that kind of takeover, moving incrementally from brutality to bondage to imprisonment, all while promising to protect and watch over.

However, what you've described doesn't sound much like a tulpa, which works through feelings. The one attacking you sounds more likely to be the category of demon that works through imagination. They're particularly status and rank-obsessed and will invent/imagine whatever non-reality they wish they were living in. That is to say, their hallmark is schizophrenic behavior including the gaslighting of others into their preferred worldview. I had an uncle with one of those — it was not pretty. I always felt generally uncomfortable around him, but on one occasion he turned and looked at me for just a split second with his demon eyes unveiled. That was a truly frightening experience! Unsurprisingly, he was hospitalized for schizophrenia and manic-depression — imagine how depressing and crazy-making it would be to have a competitive, delusionally inventive, status-obsessed demon haranguing you at every turn!

What demons do is attach etheric, astral, mental, or spiritual parasites onto others, with the goal of compromising them enough that they too will invoke a demon. We all put parasites on each other to some extent, with the weaker ones easily getting resolved each night as we sleep. What those parasites are is just fixed energy swirls of imbalance. Actually, everything is a fixed energy swirl, with humans, demons, and angels being particularly complex ones. What you've described of shouting back at something pestering you, feeling like something was tearing at you, being overcome by self-destructive desires, and getting extremely sick sounds to me like you've had some extraordinarily potent parasites attached to you. Like humans, demons are at different points along their path; some are barely getting the hang of being demonic; others are seriously training to compete in the demon Olympics. It’s kind of luck of the draw... bad luck at that!

I’m sorry to say that what you’ve described sounds to me like a frighteningly competent and malicious demon is attacking you. If swerving into oncoming traffic and feeling overcome by a desire to crash your car are entirely new experiences for you, then they are not coming from you; they are being done to you. Unless you have had a history of that kind of behavior or imbalance (pointing to a need to resolve deeper issues within you), then you are being attacked — potently attacked. What you are describing sounds like your life has been put in clear and present danger.

Your wife’s reaction to your being seriously ill as though that were a legitimate threat to her own imperiled happiness, along with accusing you of attacking her while you’re in such a weakened state sound exactly like the tactics of an imagination demon. Trying to gain points / pull rank while you’re already suffering at your nadir and gaslighting you by saying you’re at fault for her suffering, when you’re the one who’s sick, are both forms of the crazy-making, invented reality of endless power struggles that imagination demons leave in their wake.

Also, it may not be a coincidence that once you became weakened and ill your wife became strengthened and happy. Parasites are swirls or eddies of energy. Picture water going down a drain — in fact, many parasites actually do operate as drains. Far too often we put parasites on others to leech away their energy and feed off of it. Pay attention to how energized your wife becomes when you are feeling depleted versus how enervated she becomes when you feel that you have access to all your own energy. If she consistently becomes energized every time you feel your energy draining away, that would be a pretty telling sign.

Obviously, I don’t know your situation or the various players first hand, but what you wrote sounds for all the world like a demon is involved. We all end up attacking each other in myriad little ways; thankfully, we also bless each other just as casually. However, what you’re going through does not sound like a casual attack in any way. Please be careful and take whatever protective measures you know how to. If you would like, I would be happy to extend my protective blessings over you as well — just drop a response to let me know if you would like that.

— Christophe

comments.title: Death and space
comments.content:
I have been reflecting on your statement a few years ago when I believe you tried to make a ternary out of life and death, stating "space, not death, is the opposite of life". It's sat with me as a philosophical worm in my brain since.

In the context of your theory that there's some sort of cosmic eclipse going on at the moment, on the second cosmic plane, it makes a lot of sense. What I think it points to is that real death, as in a force entirely alien to the solar system, comes from without, not within, and this would be of a particular concern when the guiding function of the central sun is lost.

I suspect that Gods, and people in touch with Gods, can network around it so to speak, and the Gods and logos probably work around it perhaps by their own friendly cosmic contacts, but without that cosmic guiding function,the void, or true death, grows stronger during that time.

I also note Fortune's comments around how the Ring Chaos is not the Unmanifest. It's the response of the Unmanifest to the Ring Cosmos. This means that the rather scary void of space which reflects the Ring Chaos is not the Unmanifest, of which we can't know much at all.

I offer this because it's so easy to equate void with a universal or cosmic "bad", when the stuff we are made from ultimately and which binds us all we can't possibly fathom.

replies.title: Re: Death and space
replies.content: Interesting. Yes, I can see that.

comments.title: Cosmic Doctrine
comments.content:
Morning John
I’ve reached chapter 12 of your brilliant commentary on the cosmic doctrine and I’m little confused. The text says that each of us has a cosmic atom in the heart of our being. Then it goes on to talk about secondary atoms that are created from their tracks in space. I understand that. Where I get lost is the reference to “seed-atoms”. Are these the original cosmic atoms or the secondary atoms? Because the text goes on to state that the seed atoms are part of our form, and infers it’s the secondary atoms not the cosmic atoms, which seems a contradiction.
Another thing that puzzles me, is why the 7 planes are reversed in the Solar system so that the material plane is next to the logos? It seems odd given the solar system is supposed to mimic the cosmos in its nature.

replies.title: Re: Cosmic Doctrine
replies.content:
1) The seed atom is the first of the secondary atoms, the one formed by the tracks in space laid down by the cosmic atom. All other secondary atoms cluster around it.

2) Fortune inherited that usage from older occult writers. Yes, it's odd -- I'm not sure why the older occult writers chose to do that. Fortune had to use it to avoid confusion.

comments.title: FHR - imagining and visualisation
comments.content:
Hi!

I am studying FHR papers right now, but I have not begun the work yet.
In one of the papers a distinction is made between imagining and visualising. I find the explanations a bit unconvincing. In my case what I imagine I visualise and what I visualise I imagine.
I don't really understand the difference. Am I missing something there?
Admittedly I am a very visual person and I day dream a lot.
Many Thanks!

replies.title: Re: FHR - imagining and visualisation
replies.content:
There are two things going on here. First of all, some people get very stressed out about visualization -- they worry that they're doing it wrong if they don't get images as clear as the ones on a video game screen. Talking about imagination makes it easier for them to avoid getting bent out of shape about that. Second, it's important in magical practice not to limit yourself to visual imagination; when you visualize a scene, it helps to also imagine hearing, touching, smelling, and (where appropriate) tasting what you're imagining. Using the word "imagine" instead of "visualize" helps get that point across.

But yes, basically, it's the same thing.

comments.title: More MOE
comments.content:
Hi JMG

A couple of queries about MOE Master material and the attunement if I may? I’m just going to cut to the chase as short on time!

1. You’re speeding up the delivery of material/attunement. Am I understanding right that as long as we’ve made a start on the Master material and intend to continue you’re OK with us receiving the attunement? In my case it’s solely been the basic BOL, but I’m looking ahead and thinking how I could apply the rest.

2. I re-read all the Master material covering the BOL. It suggests things like establishing the BOL in the morning and “having it ready” for when needed. The BOL always needs to be toward a specific target though, yes? So this isn’t a case of opening the BOL in general (akin to activating one’s hands in morning but not using them)?

3. I don’t do Healing Breath, though am thinking I might have to find a way to fit it in after all as it sounds like it ties a lot of MOE together, and I’m getting a lot out of MOE….but until then can I seek reassurance that any and all mention of “healing breath” throughout material can be disregarded as long as I’m still keeping up discursive meditation?

More specifically with example of the following:

““The Essene Healing Voice may be activated by establishing yourself in the Essene Healing Breath, activating your Essene Healing Hands of Love, placing the four Elemental signs of Earth, Water, Fire and Air on your throat with your right index finger, and tapping your third eye three times with that same finger. “”

...for me, trying this practice might mean just doing some relaxation/fourfold breath prior to activation, as I think I’d like to keep it separate entirely from the discursive practice.

Hope that makes sense!

replies.title: Re: More MOE
replies.content:
1) Yes, that's correct. John Gilbert sometimes gave all three attunements to someone in a single weekend, with the understanding that the person would then go and do all the practices on their own time.

2) Once you're familiar with establishing the Bridge, you can open it with the intention of healing anyone you encounter who consents to healing. Then it's always ready. I don't recommend this unless you do a lot of healing on a daily basis, but it's an option.

3) Just substitute "fourfold breath" for "healing breath" and you'll be fine. That's what John advised me to do.

replies.content:

I actually just spent 15 mins doing the Healing Breath phase 1. Relaxing the body felt simple after all the Discursive practice. It remains to be seen if I can fit it in long term, but it doesn't seem all that demanding on balance and I definitely enjoyed.

It'd probably mean altering my plan to pick up the DMH again later this year. comments.title: MOE Master Teacher; rituals and incense
comments.content:
Good morning everybody,

And thank you very much to you, JMG, for tirelessly hosting this space and answering questions.

I‘ve got a few practical things today, and would very much appreciate your replies:

The MOE Master Teacher:

1. In taking the Master Teacher, will there be any commitments one might want to be aware of in advance? I.e. any vows, promises, …?

2. And are there any expectations from your end about what a Master Teacher should or shouldn‘t do, e.g. with regards to the actual „teaching“, passing on of attunements, etc?

replies.title: Re: MOE Master Teacher; rituals and incense
replies.content:
1) The only obligation is a request -- not an oath or anything like that! -- that you pass on the Master Teacher knowledge only to serious practitioners of the Essene methods who have completed the Gnostic Lessons.

2) Nope. You get to choose all that yourself, within the limits of the request just described.

comments.title: Children’s past lives
comments.content:
Hi JMG and others. I’m looking for some advice about past lives.

My 3-year old son started saying things a few weeks ago, such as that his “family disappeared in the woods.” That hasn’t happened to anyone we know, so it’s not something he has experienced. Although it was eyebrow-raising, it was a generic-enough tale that he could have heard something similar elsewhere. Or it could be just an active imagination.

The other day, however, he said the “red guards” took away his “lab” and his “mom,” as well as some other details he couldn’t have heard of from growing up with us (even from books, movies, or other media). When it started getting this specific, we started to take the story more seriously as a potential past life.

My question is—as a parent—what is the best course of action to take? Just affirm whatever he says and let the memories fade away naturally with time? Try to pry out as many details as we can and write it all down? Take him to some kind of past life regression therapist? He doesn’t seem distressed about the memories, so right now we’re just listening and letting him say whatever comes to his mind.

I’m aware of Ian Stevenson’s work but I’ve never read his books. From what I have read, such memories tend to come out between the ages of 3 and 7, so I would expect more details may come out as time goes on.

I just want to do the best thing for his long term mental health. Neither of us parents have any experience with this. I welcome any advice or suggestions, including from anyone else who has experience with this.

replies.title: Re: Children’s past lives
replies.content: I haven't had the experience of helping children through this kind of experience, but I know people who have. I don't recommend taking the child to a regression therapist or anything of the kind, and I don't recommend trying to push the process; simply write down every detail that comes up -- your child may want to know about all this later -- and make sure he feels accepted and loved. (Too many people respond to this kind of thing, or any paranormal experience in a child, by bullying the child into silence.) This is perfectly natural and normal; there's no risk to your child's mental health; just treat it as an ordinary part of growing up, and he'll be fine.

replies.title: Re: Children’s past lives
replies.content: Thank you, John Michael.

replies.title: Re: What does one have to do with the other?
replies.content:
One of the things that fascinates me most about the current cultural left is the enthusiasm with which it seems to be trying to conform to every extreme right-wing stereotype it can find. Have you see the attempts by people on the far left to legitimize pedophilia, or the way that so many Wiccans -- who used to reject fundamentalist claims that Wicca is devil worship -- have gone plunging straight into demonolatry of late? (I talked about that latter a while back in this Dreamwidth post.)

This is another example of the same thing. What comes next, to judge by historical parallels, is that a lot of people who currently identify as LGBTQ+ will turn on a dime, repent their sinful ways, and become devout conservative Christians, the same way that so many hippies in the wake of the Sixties became "Jesus People," then cut their hair, got conventional clothing, and settled down into the conventional lifestyles they'd briefly rejected. The devil thing is the intermediate stage, the point at which the former dissidents start conforming to the stereotype in order to facilitate their abandonment of their former beliefs.

replies.title: Re: What does one have to do with the other?
replies.content:
Perhaps "who think they can sing" is the relevant detail. Most of us know we're not that great (absolutely everybody sounds better than I do, and the occasional visiting chanters make us all look like amateurs), but we are there to learn, and whether we are any good or not learning the music is a discipline that's good for the soul-- it's not a performance, and a congregation isn't an audience: we are all participants in the service of God and each other. Plus, once you have sung things enough times, they become a special form of memory-- part of the inalienable trove of things you carry around in your soul, that pop up just when you need them. Almost like the opposite of advertising jingles, which annoyingly pop up when you don't need them. From experience: learning to sing the Psalms chases away all other ear-worms, and comes in handy in all sorts of unexpected ways in day-to-day life. That David guy knew what he was talking about.

So... it's less about native talent, and more about taking up a salutary discipline, and I encourage every new person to give it a try. And there's the added bonus that if you're as bad at it as I am, you will learn humility the way all us vain people must: through constant public humiliation ;)

comments.title: Practising death
comments.content:
JMG I’ve just ordered 4 lending copies of the Occult Philosophy Workbook and am in the process of working out an exercise for someone to consider death. Now, this someone has just turned 82 and over the last few years we have discussed death and reincarnation – they are open to the idea of reincarnation but fearful of death (i.e. didn’t want to talk about that etc). Now, granted much is metaphor, and I’ll stick a front end on the process, but is there anything in this that seems problematic or maybe something missing?

It is based on p 113, but as she is already 82, it seemed like the idea of ‘imagine yourself when you are very old’ did not quite seem apt ;)
Anyway, just playing with ideas – your exposition on the cycle of reincarnation hits the spot; is the following problematic to you?

Imagine that after a long life that you are ready for a new adventure and that you are going to leave the physical body. Smile to yourself and give thanks for the life you have had. Imagine that you are going to leave your body, sort of like taking off an old set of clothes. Now think of yourself somehow floating slowly up out of your body so you are just above it and that you turn around to look down at your physical self. Imagine that you watch your physical body as it relaxes and the final breath of material existence leaves the body’s lips. Now notice that you still feel like yourself and remember that your physical body is only one part of you. You feel like yourself because you still are you! Your soul is now just clothed in your etheric and astral bodies along with the mental sheath (the seed of your mental body) and you can feel your etheric body the way you can feel your physical body in material life.

Now open your eyes and rub your hands together, place the palms lightly over your eyes and feel the warmth from your hands relaxing your eyes.
Place your hands in your lap or place just below navel and take a few relaxed abdominal breaths.

Congratulations - You have just practised the first death… the beginning of the journey to a new life.

replies.title: Re: Practising death
replies.content:
Hmm! I like this. It seems very suitable.

It's by no means unrealistic to be a little afraid of death, btw. Most ways of dying are pretty uncomfortable and some of them are very painful indeed. I find it useful to accept that it's scary and will probably hurt, but that we're all going to go through it anyway...

comments.title: Love Request
comments.content:
1. When making a request of a planetary intelligence or just a God to find true love, how do you formulate the request? The best I've been able to come up with is "Help me to find a perfect romantic match sexually, emotionally, intellectually and spiritually, who will lead to a long and blissfully happy marriage, and help me to become worthy of such a dream girl. Let your will in this matter be done, rather than my own."

It's a bit clunky and verbose, but I'm paranoid of the "you get exactly what you ask for" stories. Is there a better way to ask to find somebody to love that leaves the door open for beings smarter than me to match me with somebody I might not otherwise consider? How would you formulate a "help me find troo wuv!" prayer/request for this?

2. Why are things like this? Language is already so mushy and vague, and the angels/intelligences/Gods we deal with are vastly far above us in understanding. So why do they act like annoying DnD rules lawyers? Why do they force us to agonize over perfectly wording a magical working when an ordinary human standing at the shrine next to us would easily understand what we meant even if we didn't say it in perfect legalese with no loopholes? Why do we get exactly what we ask for rather that what we're actually asking for?

replies.title: Re: Love Request
replies.content:
1) My approach is always to start with the self. "Help me to become more lovable, so that I will attract a woman with whom I will have a long and happy marriage" would be an example. That way you're changing the thing you can change most easily.

It's also a very, very risky thing to aspire to a perfection you haven't earned. I'd be concerned, in the case of the intention you've phrased, that the "help me to become worthy" line would guarantee you a long period of really serious misery -- because if you really want a perfect marriage, you're going to have to become a perfect person, and that means being fed through the meatgrinder until all your imperfections have been chopped away. Perfection is earned through pain; that's the harsh side of the law of karma.

2) Gods and angels are far above us in understanding. Intelligences are roughly on our level, but they're not human and don't understand human beings, and spirits aren't as smart as we are. The rule about phrasing things exactly was devised by people who did a lot of work with intelligences and spirits, and learned that you really do have to spell things out. You don't have to do that with gods and angels, but you do need to remember that they see things from an eternal point of view and so their idea of what's good for you will not be the same as yours.

It's also quite common for people to have inaccurate ideas about what they actually want. I imagine we've all known people who got what they thought they wanted, and ended up miserable because they'd misunderstood their own desires. This is another reason why effective magic doesn't aim for perfection -- instead, it aims for targeted personal improvements that remove difficulties and open opportunities.

replies.title: Re: Love Request
replies.content:
Thanks for this bit of conversation on prayer and communication with deities. I tend to feel somewhat hamstrung in my efforts to pray and never get beyond just asking for “blessing and protection “ because I fear that if I get what I think I want it’ll lead to trouble.

Other than not letting all prayer be a request for something, is there any way to get beyond the sense of walking on karmic eggshells? Sometimes it seems the only alternative is to keep bumbling along and hoping my efforts at self improvement fend off catastrophe.

replies.title: Re: Love Request
replies.content: Remember that the gods are very patient with human bumbling; they know us a lot better than we know ourselves. The first step, as you've already hinted, is to conceive of prayer as a matter of building a relationship rather than getting goodies; you're talking to a wise and powerful person who may be interested in becoming your friend. As you build the relationship, what you should and shouldn't do will become increasingly clear.

comments.title: Names of Power
comments.content:
Hello, Mr. Greer,

As someone who knows little about magic, and its theory and practice, I was wondering if you could help clarify the technology of names? I’m speaking of knowing a thing’s or god’s name in order to access some power over it.
I have found that knowing Gurdjieff’s technical name for astral light, as well as understanding somewhat its function and relationship with other matters has given me a degree of facility with it, I believe; especially after studying some of the Fourth Way charts.

Probably, knowing a name is not enough in itself, but understanding a thing’s nature is very important too? If I chose to improve my relationship with a god, would knowing other names for him/her help in that? How else could I improve my relationship with a god?

Thank you, as always.

replies.title: Re: Names of Power
replies.content:
A true name is a symbol, not just a label. It's not arbitrary; it expresses some otherwise incommunicable truth about what it names. Knowing the name is just the beginning; meditating on it, understanding it, studying it, resonating with it, chanting it a thousand times in ritual -- all this turns it into a manifestation of the being that it names.

As for how else you can improve your relationship with a god, why, I hear there's this thing called prayer, which helps... ;-)

comments.title: Animal Sacrifice
comments.content: Reading through Picatrix, and looking at some of the operations, such as one where you behead a ram, have a feast, invoke a planet... I'm theoretically not against animal sacrifice at all - it's just sharing a meal with a powerful, non-corporeal, friend. I have harvested chickens, geese, deer, rabbits &ct, and I eat meat. However, part of me is still more than a little circumspect, partially due to the sentiment expressed by you in an above reply. Do you have any advice in this regard? Maybe getting in touch with a practitioner of Santeria, say?

replies.title: Re: Animal Sacrifice
replies.content: I really, seriously recommend against any involvement in animal sacrifice. Unless you know what you're doing and have adequate training -- the sort of thing that a Jewish shochet (kosher butcher) or Vodoun priest has -- you run the risk of attracting entities of a kind you really don't want to deal with. In the late classical world, theurgists found that you can get the same effect through prayer and burning incense; people like Proclus and Iamblichus invoked planetary deities that way with excellent results, and I've found this highly effective.

replies.title: Re: Animal Sacrifice
replies.content: Are these entities more attracted to a sacrifice (where beneficial entities are invoked) than to a regular harvesting of an animal (where no beneficial entities are invoked, just the spirit of the animal thanked)?

replies.title: Re: Animal Sacrifice
replies.content: It varies according to circumstances. By all accounts industrial abbatoirs are horrific to see on the inner planes.
